negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing regular provider cache TTL/idle environment configuration. The port uses ExecutionCache/CachedStateProvider fixed-size caches and has no RETH_STATE_CACHE_TTL_SECS/RETH_STATE_CACHE_IDLE_SECS handling or 7200s/3600s defaults.
related_but_insufficient_code: CachedStateProvider and ExecutionCache exist, but their creation paths pass cache size rather than time_to_live/time_to_idle.

rationale: The assigned candidate captures the load-bearing environment configurability and application to storage/account/code caches. A bounded current-file check shows the default values were later changed from 300/300 seconds to 7200/3600 seconds, so the refined invariant keeps the current behavior but lowers confidence because that later change is not represented by a separate candidate in this packet.
statement: Regular provider account, storage, and code caches read RETH_STATE_CACHE_TTL_SECS for time_to_live and RETH_STATE_CACHE_IDLE_SECS for time_to_idle when the variables parse as u64 seconds; at current tip invalid or missing values fall back to 7200 seconds TTL and 3600 seconds idle, and pinned system-contract caches are not given TTL/idle expiry.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set RETH_STATE_CACHE_TTL_SECS/IDLE_SECS and assert account/storage/code caches expire on those durations; current code has no such inputs.
summary: Regular state cache expiry env configuration is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Search and unit test for cache env configuration; currently absent.
title: Regular state cache expiry is environment-configurable
